name: SolarMax Technology, Inc.
business: SolarMax Technology is an integrated solar and renewable energy company. Its primary business consists of the sale and installation of photovoltaic and battery backup systems for residential and commercial customers, sales of LED systems and services to government and commercial users, and since the third quarter of 2025, EPC services for the construction of BESS systems.
namedCustomers: State Power Investment Corporation Guizhou Jinyuan Weining Energy Co., Ltd (SPIC)

exposures.sentence: Since the third quarter of 2025, our primary business has been negotiating contracts and performing EPC services for solar-based BESS commercial systems.
We are an integrated solar and renewable energy company.
Since the third quarter of 2025, our principal business was EPC services in connection with the construction of BESS systems.
We provide LED products that help commercial customers save money by lowering electricity costs through the advanced technology of LED light bulbs.

We may be subject to cybersecurity risks. We face significant and persistent cybersecurity risks due to the need to protect both our business generally and any transaction which we are considering or with respect to which we are in negotiation, as well as the need to protect the confidentiality of information concerning our personnel and others with whom we conduct business.

catalysts.sentence: Completion of the BESS facility is targeted for June 2026, although we cannot assure you that this completion date will be met.

catalysts.event: Execution of three EPC agreements for BESS systems
catalysts.sentence: On December 31, 2025, we entered into three EPC agreements for large scale BESS systems, two in Puerto Rico and one in Corpus Christi, Texas.

In March 2024, we issued 5,039,950 shares of common stock in our initial public offering at a public offering price of $4.00 per share less a 6% underwriting discount pursuant to an underwriting agreement.
On July 31, 2025, SREP entered into an EPC agreement (the Longfellow Contract) with Longfellow, a Texas limited liability company, for an industrial project to develop a BESS facility.

tamClaims.claim: The U.S. solar market is expected to be valued at over $125 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: The U.S. solar market is expected to be valued at over $125 billion by 2030 as investment in large-scale utility projects and distributed generation continues to expand.
